posts.hook: Sunday reset: 5-minute eye workout to start your week with clear vision.
posts.caption:
Sunday reset for your eyes.

5-minute routine:
1. Palming (90s) — warm hands, cover eyes, breathe
2. Focus Shift (60s) — near to far, 10 reps
3. Figure Eight (60s) — trace a figure 8 with your eyes
4. 20-20-20 cool down (30s)

Do this every Sunday. Your Monday eyes will feel different.

Free at axeo.vision/exercise

#SundayReset #EyeWorkout #Axeo #AxeoVision #HealthTok #SelfCareSunday
posts.visual_notes: Calm, clean aesthetic. Follow-along format with timer for each exercise. Soft ambient music. Sunday morning vibes — natural light, cozy setting.

posts.caption:

posts.hook: Week 1 recap: everything we posted about eye health this week.
posts.caption:
Week 1 recap — here's what you learned about your eyes this week:

- Your blink rate drops 60% on screens
- 1 in 12 men have color vision deficiency
- Eye exercises ARE backed by clinical research
- Kids are getting myopia at 2x the rate of 20 years ago
- Weekend screen time spikes 30-40%

Which fact surprised you the most? Comment below.

posts.hook: Weekly discussion: What's your screen time average and do you notice eye strain?
posts.caption:
Sunday check-in for r/eyehealth —

Curious about the correlation between screen time and eye strain symptoms in this community.

A few questions:
1. What's your average daily screen time? (Check your phone's report)
2. Do you experience any recurring symptoms? (dry eyes, headaches, blurry distance vision)
3. Have you tried any exercises or habits that actually helped?

I've been doing 5 minutes of structured eye exercises daily (focus shifting + palming) and tracking symptoms. Definitely noticed less end-of-day fatigue after 2 weeks.

Using axeo.vision/exercise for guided routines if anyone wants to try the same thing.

Would love to compare notes.
posts.visual_notes: Text post to r/eyehealth. Discussion format, inviting community engagement. No images needed.
